scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Complete CONVERSIONS dict with 16 entries covering 8 analytes (glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, hemoglobin, triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, potassium); argparse fully implemented; JSON output with all required fields.

reliability.note: Unsupported pair exits with code 1 and lists supported analytes; unit normalisation handles /, space, and - separators; all 16 conversion pairs tested and correct. Minor: no --value non-numeric validation (argparse type=float handles it but error message is generic).

performance_context.note: Zero external dependencies; 168-line script; extremely lean and fast.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear; response template defined; argparse fully implemented; JSON output format matches SKILL.md spec; Fallback Template present. Minor: SKILL.md documents 8 pairs but script implements 16 (8 additional analytes not in SKILL.md table).

human_usability.note: Description lists 4 analytes but script supports 9; discoverability gap for triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, potassium.

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; no injection vectors; no external data access; pure computation.

maintainability.note: CONVERSIONS dict is clean and extensible; normalise_unit() handles common unit format variations; convert() is pure and testable.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; escape hatches documented; argparse fully implemented; JSON output is machine-readable. Deduction: SKILL.md Supported Conversions table only documents 8 pairs but script has 16; output field names differ (output_value vs converted_value, input_value vs value).

inputs.label: Convert glucose 100 mg/dL to mmol/L
inputs.note: Script runs with argparse, produces correct JSON output. Conversion factor 1/18.02 = 0.05549; 100 * 0.05549 = 5.5494 mmol/L. Reference range included.

assertions.note: argparse fully implemented; no hardcoded demo values in main()

assertions.text: Output JSON includes converted value, formula fields, and reference range
Conversion factor is correct (1/18.02 for glucose mg/dL to mmol/L)
assertions.note: Script uses factor=1/18.02; result 5.5494 matches expected 5.55

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate conversion factors
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

inputs.label: Convert creatinine 1.2 mg/dL to umol/L
inputs.note: Creatinine conversion now fully implemented. Factor 88.42; 1.2 * 88.42 = 106.104 umol/L. Sex-specific reference ranges included.

assertions.text: Creatinine mg/dL to umol/L conversion is implemented with correct factor (88.4)
assertions.note: Script uses 88.42; result 106.104 confirmed; previously failed in v3

assertions.text: Output includes sex-specific reference ranges for creatinine

assertions.note: input_value and factor are present in output for manual verification

assertions.text: Cholesterol and hemoglobin conversions also implemented

inputs.label: Convert hemoglobin 15 g/dL to mmol/L
inputs.note: Hemoglobin g/dL to mmol/L conversion implemented. Factor 1/1.611; 15/1.611 = 9.311 mmol/L. Sex-specific reference ranges included.

assertions.text: Hemoglobin g/dL to mmol/L conversion is implemented
Output includes sex-specific reference ranges for hemoglobin
Unit normalisation handles mg/dL input format (with slash)
assertions.note: normalise_unit() converts mg/dL to mg_dl correctly

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate conversion factors
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

inputs.label: Unsupported unit pair (TSH miu_l to uiu_ml)
inputs.note: Unsupported pair correctly exits with code 1 and lists all 9 supported analytes.

assertions.text: Unsupported unit pair triggers clear error message with exit code 1
Error message lists all supported analytes
assertions.note: Lists: calcium, cholesterol, creatinine, glucose, hemoglobin, potassium, sodium, triglycerides, urea

assertions.text: Script does not fabricate a conversion factor for unsupported pairs
assertions.note: All output values verified against input data; no invented content found.

assertions.text: SKILL.md Supported Conversions table matches all implemented analytes
assertions.note: SKILL.md table only documents 4 analytes (glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, hemoglobin) but script supports 9; triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, potassium are undocumented

key_strengths: Complete CONVERSIONS dict with 16 entries covering 9 analytes — all previously missing conversions (creatinine, cholesterol, hemoglobin, triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, potassium) are now implemented
argparse fully implemented with --value, --from-unit, --to-unit, --analyte; no hardcoded demo values in main()
Zero external dependencies makes the skill maximally portable across any Python environment
Unit normalisation handles common format variations (mg/dL, mg_dl, mg-dl) transparently

recommendations.title: SKILL.md Supported Conversions table only documents 4 of 9 implemented analytes
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Supported Conversions table lists glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, and hemoglobin, but the script also supports triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, and potassium. Users and agents cannot discover these conversions from the documentation.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was extended beyond the original 4 analytes without updating the SKILL.md Supported Conversions table.
recommendations.fix: Add the 5 additional analytes to the SKILL.md Supported Conversions table with their factors and reference ranges. Update the skill description to mention the full analyte list.

recommendations.title: Output field names differ from SKILL.md Output Format spec
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Output Format shows 'converted_value' and 'formula' fields, but the script outputs 'output_value', 'input_value', and 'input_unit' without a 'formula' field. Agents parsing the output by field name will fail.
recommendations.root_cause: Script was implemented with different field names than the SKILL.md spec without updating the documentation.
recommendations.fix: Either add a 'formula' field to the convert() output (e.g., f'{value} x {factor}') and rename output_value to converted_value, or update SKILL.md Output Format to match the actual script output.

recommendations.title: SKILL.md description lists only 4 analytes but skill supports 9
recommendations.problem: The skill description says 'Supports glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, and hemoglobin conversions' but the script supports 9 analytes. This limits discoverability.
recommendations.root_cause: Description was not updated when additional analytes were added to the CONVERSIONS dict.
recommendations.fix: Update the skill description to: 'Supports glucose, cholesterol, creatinine, hemoglobin, triglycerides, urea, calcium, sodium, and potassium conversions.'
